what: Timeouts and contention in parallel mode
title: Configuration
text: mutant run wall-clock timeout; exceeding reported HANG. timeout derived long package’s test suite takes, unless pass timeout_seconds explicitly. mutants run parallel (cores time), timeout must account contention: many workers, test run slower alone packages load many dependencies, heavy per-mutant install work. timeout based single, uncontended baseline run fire nearly every mutant (leading numerous false HANGs). avoid , mutate_package() first runs baseline suite (confirm passes fail fast otherwise), runs cores times concurrently takes slowest contended baseline. timeout max(contended_baseline * 1.5, 5s). self-calibrates machine, chosen parallelism, package’s real load/compile cost, avoiding manual tuning. Pass timeout_seconds override entirely. cores = 1, forking unavailable, timing baseline suite used.

title: Configuration
text: default mutate_package() runs mutant’s tests CRAN mode (cran = TRUE): NOT_CRAN environment variable set "false" test subprocess, testthat::skip_on_cran() skip_if_offline() guards take effect mutator runs tests CRAN . skips slow, flaky, network-dependent tests packages mark CRAN-skippable, keeps mutation runs fast avoids spurious timeouts/kills , e.g., tests hit network. Set cran = FALSE run full suite instead (NOT_CRAN = "true", happens default running devtools::test()): applies test strategies (testthat strategy installed-tests fallback). Note affects tests package explicitly guards skip_on_cran() / skip_if_offline(); package whose network test guard still run test either mode.
code: mutate_package("path/to/pkg", cran = FALSE)

what: Fail-fast (stop at the first failing test)
title: Configuration
text: mutant KILLED instant tests detects , running rest suite wasted work. default (fail_fast = TRUE), mutant’s test run stops first failing test instead finishing suite, speeds test-running phase (often substantially packages large suites) without changing mutant’s verdict. Set fail_fast = FALSE run full suite every mutant: applies testthat strategy: sets TESTTHAT_MAX_FAILS = 1 test subprocess uses progress reporter, aborts run first failing test_that() block. SURVIVED mutants unaffected, failure short-circuit , baseline timing timeout calibration unchanged. installed-tests fallback already stops first failing test file regardless flag.
code: mutate_package("path/to/pkg", fail_fast = FALSE)

title: Configuration
text: default, mutant package copy symlinks unchanged directories original package mutated R/ file materialised, parallel workers point physical src/, tests/, etc. fast, two design choices keep correct: Compiled code built , per mutant. testthat strategy, pkgload::load_all() reuses baseline’s compiled src/ since C code never mutated. installedstrategy compiles template library installs mutant ---libs, never writes src/. prevents parallel workers fighting compiled objects. Snapshot references shared. testthat packages _snaps directories, mutator gives mutant snapshot copy symlinking rest tests/, filtered parallel runs rewrite shared snapshot references. one remaining hazard non-hermetic tests write files shared directory (often tests/). parallel workers’ tests fight files can still see spurious KILLED/HANG. Two ways attenuate : Run without parallelism: cores = 1. contention, extra disk, slowest option. Isolate file state: isolate = TRUE. mutant gets deep copy src/ tests/ instead symlink, file-writing tests can’t collide: default (isolate = FALSE) fast correct hermetic test suites; reach isolate = TRUE (cores = 1) package’s tests hermetic see parallel-KILLED/HANG results.
code: mutate_package("path/to/pkg", isolate = TRUE)

title: Configuration
text: optional pbmcapply package installed, multi-core test run shows live progress bar.
code: install.packages("pbmcapply")

title: Configuration
text: code mutation-tested. Vendored/standalone files, generated code, deprecated paths suite meant cover mostly produce SURVIVED mutants depress score without telling anything useful. ways exclude code. 1. file, call site, exclude_files. character vector shell-style glob patterns matched base names .R files R/. Matching files skipped entirely mutants generated: 2. source, # mutator:ignore directives. Place markers .R file : # mutator:ignore-file anywhere file excludes whole file. # mutator:ignore-start / # mutator:ignore-end exclude line region (wrap function exclude ): unmatched -start excludes end file. 3. covr’s # nocov annotations. mutator also honours covr’s coverage-exclusion comments, code already marked untested--design needs separate mutator directive: # nocov start / # nocov end exclude line region . bare # nocov excludes line, may trail code: 4. covr .covrignore file. package root .covrignore (covr’s file-level coverage-exclusion list), mutator reads : line glob expanded relative package root (matched directory expands files ), matching R/ files skipped generation, using mechanism covr uses. files already exclude coverage need extra mutator configuration. Granularity. Excluding whole files whole functions reliable. fine-grained , operator mutations: R mostly attaches source references blocks {}, engine resolves operator mutant’s position enclosing block. region directive inside function therefore excludes function’s operator mutants group: single one operator mid-function. Line-deletion mutants excluded line-precisely. practice, wrap whole functions, fragments. affects surviving mutant output, shows larger context necessary, prevents coverage-guided test selection precise . granularity can improved optional imputesrcref package.
code: mutate_package("path/to/scales", exclude_files = c("import-standalone-*")) # mutator:ignore-start legacy_helper <- function(x) {   # not worth mutation-testing   x * 2 } # mutator:ignore-end if (impossible_state) {   stop("unreachable") # nocov }

title: Configuration
text: mutants settled small subset suite, mutant line test exercises can never killed. coverage_guided = TRUE (default) mutator measures coverage covr , mutant, runs test files cover mutated line, skips running tests altogether mutants uncovered lines (reported SURVIVED immediately). Coverage guidance applies testthat strategy . resolved strategy installed-tests fallback, mutator attribute coverage test files, emits warning runs full suite every mutant. Pass coverage_guided = FALSE disable optimisation (warning). single coverage run also serves baseline check (runs package’s tests/testthat.R harness, fails test fails), suite run twice. Selection test-file level, testthat filters tests file, assumes suite deterministically exercises code, changes tests run without changing mutant’s verdict. Requires covr package. Coverage attribution (therefore speed-) depends backend (coverage_backend): "record_tests" (default) uses covr’s record_tests single run relies covr’s public output. limitation: covr credits covered line deepest test-directory frame call stack, test reaches package code function defined helper-*.R / setup-*.R file (common pattern), covr attributes helper, originating test-*.R. true triggering test unknown, mutator conservatively runs full suite mutant. Packages wrap API shared test helpers therefore see less speed-. "per_file" instruments package runs suite single time reporter snapshots coverage per test file, giving exact file-level attribution helper fallback, roughly cost single record_tests run (often faster overall, since mutants get narrowed test set). reaches covr internals, opt-: Either way pay-largest suite big, many lines uncovered, tests exercise code directly.
code: # On by default; pass FALSE to run the full suite for every mutant. mutate_package("path/to/pkg", coverage_guided = FALSE) mutate_package("path/to/pkg", coverage_guided = TRUE, coverage_backend = "per_file")

title: Configuration
text: reported mutant carries source Range: (start:col-end:col). statement- line-deletion mutants precise, operator mutants (+, <, &&, …) different: R attaches srcref nested call objects, engine can report bounds enclosing block ({}), effectively loop body even whole function. surviving ==--!= mutant 40-line function therefore points 40 lines. optional imputesrcref package installed, mutator uses recover precise spans many operator mutants, typically narrowing whole-function range exact sub-expression single line. GitHub-optional package listed Enhances; install opt : installed, mutator behaves exactly (coarser operator ranges); nothing else changes. refinement used read-source-location oracle: mutant files deparsed original code byte--byte identical whether imputesrcref present, # mutator:ignore-* directives keep function-granular semantics regardless. best results, install package test source parse data retained, since imputesrcref reads :
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("PRL-PRG/imputesrcref") install.packages("<pkg>", INSTALL_opts = c("--with-keep.source", "--with-keep.parse.data"))

title: Configuration
text: Equivalent-mutant detection calls OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions API. Configure ways (listed highest precedence first; setting resolved independently, can mixed): Programmatically, R session: .openai_config file working directory (directory pass via get_openai_config(dir = ...)). plain, human-readable file field: value lines parsed, never executed: given directory consulted. Parent directories searched. Environment variables OPENAI_API_KEY, OPENAI_MODEL OPENAI_BASE_URL. nothing configured, model defaults gpt-4 base URL public OpenAI API. Set base_url target self-hosted alternative OpenAI-compatible service (example http://localhost:11434/v1). Enable analysis running package mutation tests: Equivalence detection runs test suites. equivalent mutant behaviorally identical original, test can kill , running test suite wasted work. mutate_package() therefore analyzes every generated mutant front skips test run judged equivalent: recorded survived directly. Mutants judged NOT_EQUIVALENT DONT_KNOW tested usual. trade-equivalence pass now covers mutants, survivors, makes API calls; exchange avoids far expensive test runs equivalent mutants. Mutants analyzed bounded batches (default 25 per request), mutant shown model small unified diff edit (plus short change label) rather full mutated source. compact, unambiguous, format LLMs read natively. mutate_package() runs cores > 1 batches sent concurrently, (bounded size) keeps equivalence pass fast avoids truncated responses otherwise drop verdicts. mutants model flags EQUIVALENT (excluded adjusted mutation score), also returns one-sentence reason, stored equivalence_reason mutant call can audited. reason requested NOT_EQUIVALENT/DONT_KNOW, keeping responses small. Progress results summary emitted via message() (can silenced suppressMessages()). full prompts model responses printed default; set options(mutator.verbose = TRUE) log .
code: set_openai_config(   api_key  = "api-key...",   model    = "gpt-4",   base_url = "https://api.openai.com/v1" # any OpenAI-compatible endpoint ) api_key: api-key... model: gpt-4 base_url: https://api.openai.com/v1 mutate_package("path/to/pkg", detectEqMutants = TRUE)

title: Continuous integration
text: mutator provides reusable workflow PRL-PRG/mutator/.github/workflows/mutation-testing.yaml. repository adopts short caller workflow rather copying script. workflow checks package, installs together mutator dependencies, runs mutate_package(), uploads results artifact, writes Markdown summary run’s Summary tab. Add .github/workflows/mutation-testing.yaml repository: Pin released tag @v0.1.0. reusable workflow lives mutator package repository versioned package, tag pin matches mutator release. Bump tag caller want move newer release. can also pin branch (example @main) track latest changes, cost reproducibility.
code: on:   pull_request:   push:     branches: [main, master]   workflow_dispatch:  name: mutation-testing  jobs:   mutation:     uses: PRL-PRG/mutator/.github/workflows/mutation-testing.yaml@v0.1.0     with:       target-margin: "0.10"

title: Continuous integration
text: inputs optional.

title: Continuous integration
text: Set fail-percentage job fail. workflow computes mutation score, threshold job exits non-zero, blocks pull request: Two points make threshold gate reliable: Reduce sampling noise. score estimated sample mutants, run--run variation exists. Use tighter target-margin (example "0.05") large max-mutants gate fail-, estimate cross threshold chance. half-width choose roughly far reported score can sit true score. Ratchet threshold. Start fail-, slightly , current score, raise add tests. locks progress without failing build day adopt . Leave fail-empty report score every run without failing. measured score, confidence interval, killed, survived, hanged counts appear run’s Summary tab uploaded artifact regardless whether threshold set.
code: jobs:   mutation:     uses: PRL-PRG/mutator/.github/workflows/mutation-testing.yaml@v0.1.0     with:       fail-under: "75"       target-margin: "0.05"

title: Continuous integration
text: Set deploy-badge: true grant caller job contents: write. workflow writes shields.io endpoint JSON file deploys gh-pages (badge-branch): Reference badge README, replacing OWNER/REPO: badge labelled mutator; message shows score , run sampled mutants, compact asymmetric confidence interval 83.2% -5.2/+4.7% (95% CI). badge colour tracks score: green 80%, yellow 60%, orange 40%, red . Set show-confidence-interval: false show mutation score badge job summary. confidence interval remains part uploaded mutation results.
code: jobs:   mutation:     permissions:       contents: write     uses: PRL-PRG/mutator/.github/workflows/mutation-testing.yaml@v0.1.0     with:       deploy-badge: true ![mutator](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/OWNER/REPO/gh-pages/mutation-score.json)

title: Continuous integration
text: workflow reads GITHUB_TOKEN install packages GitHub, provided automatically. need set permissions: contents: write caller job deploy-badge true, publishing badge pushes branch. Pull request runs never deploy badge. package depends packages CRAN, pass extra-packages pak specs:
code: with:       extra-packages: "github::owner/dependency, any::somePackage"

title: Continuous integration
text: Mutation testing runs test suite per mutant, large package can take . keep CI fast: Cap work max-mutants, let target-margin size sample . coverage-guided default runs tests cover mutated line. applies packages tested testthat; installed-tests packages workflow warns runs full suite. Use exclude-files skip generated vendored files R/. Raise cores larger runner, leave "0" use every core. See Configuration article options behave detail.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Pierre Donat-Bouillud. Author, maintainer, copyright holder. Assanali Amandykov. Author, copyright holder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Donat-Bouillud P, Amandykov (2026). mutator: Mutation Testing 'R'. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/PRL-PRG/mutator.
code: @Manual{,   title = {mutator: Mutation Testing for 'R'},   author = {Pierre Donat-Bouillud and Assanali Amandykov},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/PRL-PRG/mutator}, }

title: Mutation Testing for R
text: mutator automated mutation testing tool R language. applies mutation testing principles help developers improve test suite quality introducing small, systematic changes (mutations) source code verifying tests can detect changes. instance, imagine following function f package: mutator generate several mutants, including: mutant, > replaced <. test suite catch change, indicates tests may comprehensive enough. mutator compute many mutants survives, .e. test suite fail mutant, many mutants killed, .e. test suite fails mutant. ratio killed mutants total mutants called mutation score measure test suite effectiveness.
code: f <- function(x) {   if (x > 0) {     return(x + 1)   } else {     return(x - 1)   } } f <- function(x) {   if (x < 0) { # Mutated comparison operator     return(x + 1)   } else {     return(x - 1)   } }

title: Mutation Testing for R
text: Comprehensive Mutation Testing: Applies various mutation operators R source code AST-Based Mutation: Uses Abstract Syntax Tree analysis intelligent code mutations Parallel Test Execution: Runs tests parallel improved performance Coverage-guided Test Selection: Runs tests cover mutated lines, also improved performance Configurable Test Harness: Supports testthat non-testthat test layouts Timeout Management: Automatically calibrates timeouts mutant test runs prevent hangs Annotations Exclusions: Allows developers exclude specific files code sections mutation testing Equivalent Mutant Detection: Identify mutants functionally equivalent original code (currently, using LLMs) Detailed Reporting: Provides mutation scores analysis test suite effectiveness

title: Mutation Testing for R
code: # Install from GitHub; package dependencies are installed automatically # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("PRL-PRG/mutator")  # Or install from local source, in an R console # install.packages("devtools") setwd("path/to/mutator") devtools::install()

title: Mutation Testing for R
text: mutator, addition show number generated mutants, surviving ones, mutation score, returns invisible list four components, list generated mutants, list mutant outcomes, list phase durations, summary mutation testing run. Mutant outcomes reported : SURVIVED: tests passed mutant KILLED: tests failed (execution error) HANG: mutant exceeded timeout See pkgdown reference full argument return-value documentation.
code: library(mutator)  # Mutate a single file mutants <- mutate_file("path/to/your/file.R")  # Optional: cap returned mutants by random selection mutants <- mutate_file("path/to/your/file.R", max_mutants = 20)  # Mutate an entire package and run tests result <- mutate_package("path/to/your/package")  # Optional: cap tested mutants across the whole package result <- mutate_package("path/to/your/package", max_mutants = 100)  # Optional: set a fixed timeout (seconds) per mutant test run result <- mutate_package("path/to/your/package", timeout_seconds = 60)  # Optional: control where mutant files are written result <- mutate_package("path/to/your/package", mutation_dir = tempdir())

title: Mutation Testing for R
text: mutator ships reusable workflow R-package repository can run mutation testing CI without copying scripts. Add caller workflow .github/workflows/mutation-testing.yaml: Pin released tag @v0.1.0; workflow versioned mutator package, tag matches package version. Set deploy-badge: true (contents: write permission) publish shields.io badge. See Continuous integration article every input, threshold guidance, badge setup.
code: on:   pull_request:   push:     branches: [main, master]  name: mutation-testing  jobs:   mutation:     uses: PRL-PRG/mutator/.github/workflows/mutation-testing.yaml@v0.1.0     with:       target-margin: "0.10"   # sample to +/-10 percentage points       fail-under: "75"        # fail CI below a 75% mutation score

title: Mutation Testing for R
text: mutator selects package test strategy automatically: tests/testthat/ exists, mutator loads mutant -process pkgload::load_all() mirrors package’s tests/testthat.R harness forwarding extractable arguments (notably filter) testthat::test_check() testthat::test_dir(), without paying install per mutant. Otherwise, tests/ exists, mutator falls back tools::testInstalledPackage(..., types = "tests") installing mutant --install-tests. fallback path supports non-testthat layouts (example tinytest-driven packages run tests/ scripts).

title: Mutation Testing for R
text: mutate_package() exposes number options control mutants run, tests selected, results refined. covered depth Configuration article: Timeouts contended baseline: per-mutant HANG timeout self-calibrated parallelism-aware baseline, timeout_seconds override . CRAN mode (cran): run tests CRAN (skipping guarded slow/network tests) full suite. Fail-fast (fail_fast): stop mutant’s run first failing test. Parallel execution isolation (isolate, cores): symlink-vs-copy package tree handle non-hermetic tests, plus optional pbmcapply progress bar. Excluding code mutation: exclude_files, -source # mutator:ignore-* directives, covr # nocov annotations, .covrignore. Coverage-guided test selection (coverage_guided, coverage_backend): default, runs tests cover mutated line (testthat strategy; warns runs full suite otherwise). Precise mutant locations: optional imputesrcref package narrower operator-mutant source ranges. Equivalent mutant detection (detectEqMutants): configuring OpenAI-compatible API used flag equivalent mutants.

title: Mutation Testing for R
text: mutator currently generates mutation families:

title: Mutation Testing for R
text: mutator depends : pkgload: loading mutated package copies testthat: test execution xml2: running C++ tests testthat::run_cpp_tests() covr: coverage-guided test selection (coverage_guided = TRUE) future furrr: parallel execution callr: subprocess test execution hard timeouts R6: per_file coverage backend reporter httr jsonlite: OpenAI API integration cli: progress bars user feedback pkgbuild: building package copies mutation testing pbmcapply: progress bars parallel execution imputesrcref: precise mutant source ranges (optional) LinkingTo: testthat (Catch2 C++ test headers) C++17: native mutation engine implementation

title: Mutation Testing for R
text: mutator uses testthat R tests testthat + Catch2 C++ tests. C++ tests located src/test-*.cpp C++ test runner src/test-runner.cpp C++ tests executed tests/testthat/test-cpp.R via run_cpp_tests("mutator") Run full test suite :

title: Get OpenAI API configuration — get_openai_config
code: get_openai_config(dir = getwd())

title: Get OpenAI API configuration — get_openai_config
text: dir Directory search .openai_config file. directory consulted (parent directories ). Defaults current working directory; pass NULL ignore config files.

title: Get OpenAI API configuration — get_openai_config
text: list elements api_key, model, base_url max_parallel_requests (integer cap concurrent API requests, NA cap).

title: Get OpenAI API configuration — get_openai_config
text: values set set_openai_config(); .openai_config file dir (human-readable "field: value" file parsed, never executed); environment variables OPENAI_API_KEY, OPENAI_MODEL, OPENAI_BASE_URL OPENAI_MAX_PARALLEL_REQUESTS; built-defaults (model "gpt-4", public OpenAI base URL).

title: Get OpenAI API configuration — get_openai_config
code: config <- get_openai_config() names(config) #> [1] "api_key"               "model"                 "base_url"              #> [4] "max_parallel_requests"

what: Identify equivalent mutants using OpenAI API — identify_equivalent_mutants
title: Identify equivalent mutants using OpenAI API — identify_equivalent_mutants
text: Analyzes survived mutants determine functionally equivalent original code using OpenAI's language models.

title: Identify equivalent mutants using OpenAI API — identify_equivalent_mutants
code: identify_equivalent_mutants(   src_file,   survived_mutants,   api_config = NULL,   batch_size = 25,   workers = 1,   report = TRUE )

title: Identify equivalent mutants using OpenAI API — identify_equivalent_mutants
text: src_file Path original source file survived_mutants List mutants survived test execution api_config Optional API configuration (loaded NULL) batch_size Maximum number mutants sent single API request. Smaller batches keep response short enough avoid truncation (silently drops verdicts) let batches run concurrently. Defaults 25. workers Number API requests run concurrently (requires forking platform). Defaults 1 (sequential). report Whether print per-file progress equivalence summary console. Defaults TRUE. mutate_package() sets FALSE running many files parallel, can print one aggregated summary (single progress bar) instead one per batch.

title: Identify equivalent mutants using OpenAI API — identify_equivalent_mutants
text: Updated list survived mutants equivalence information

title: Identify equivalent mutants using OpenAI API — identify_equivalent_mutants
code: src <- tempfile(fileext = ".R") writeLines("add <- function(x, y) x + y", src) survived <- list(mutant_001 = list(mutation_info = "x + y -> x - y")) suppressWarnings(identify_equivalent_mutants(     src,     survived,     api_config = list(api_key = "", model = "gpt-4") )) #> $mutant_001 #> $mutant_001$mutation_info #> [1] "x + y -> x - y" #>  #>

what: Generate Mutants for a Single R File — mutate_file
title: Generate Mutants for a Single R File — mutate_file
text: Creates mutants single R source file combining AST-based mutations C++ mutation engine fallback line-deletion mutants.

title: Generate Mutants for a Single R File — mutate_file
code: mutate_file(   src_file,   out_dir = "mutations",   max_mutants = NULL,   max_line_deletions = 5 )

title: Generate Mutants for a Single R File — mutate_file
text: src_file Path R source file. out_dir Directory mutant files written. max_mutants Optional cap number returned mutants. set, random subset generated mutants returned. max_line_deletions Maximum number line-deletion mutants generated per file (random subset deletable lines). complement AST-based statement deletions also covering top-level / non-block lines. Use 0 disable line-deletion mutants entirely. Defaults 5.

title: Generate Mutants for a Single R File — mutate_file
text: list mutants. element contains: path Path mutant file. info Formatted mutation metadata (file, source range, details). loc Machine-readable location: list file_path, start_line, end_line (latter two NA unavailable).

title: Generate Mutants for a Single R File — mutate_file
code: src <- tempfile(fileext = ".R") writeLines("add <- function(x, y) x + y", src) mutants <- mutate_file(src, out_dir = tempfile("mutations_"), max_mutants = 1) #> Generated 1 AST-based mutants for file1aab3ecd7901.R length(mutants) #> [1] 1

what: Run Mutation Testing for an R Package — mutate_package
title: Run Mutation Testing for an R Package — mutate_package
text: Mutates .R files package's R/ directory, runs package's tests mutant parallel, summarizes mutation outcomes.

title: Run Mutation Testing for an R Package — mutate_package
code: mutate_package(   pkg_dir,   cores = max(1, parallel::detectCores() - 2),   isFullLog = FALSE,   detectEqMutants = FALSE,   mutation_dir = NULL,   max_mutants = NULL,   timeout_seconds = NULL,   config_dir = getwd(),   max_line_deletions = 0,   cran = TRUE,   fail_fast = TRUE,   isolate = FALSE,   exclude_files = NULL,   strategy = c("auto", "testthat", "installed"),   coverage_guided = TRUE,   coverage_backend = c("record_tests", "per_file"),   target_margin = NULL,   confidence = 0.95,   max_show = 50L )

title: Run Mutation Testing for an R Package — mutate_package
text: pkg_dir Path package directory. cores Number parallel workers used mutant test execution. isFullLog Logical; TRUE, prints per-mutant logs timeout info. detectEqMutants Logical; TRUE, every generated mutant analyzed equivalence using OpenAI-based workflow test suites run. Mutants judged equivalent recorded survived without running tests, test can kill equivalent mutant; remaining mutants tested usual. mutation_dir Optional directory store generated mutant files. NULL, temporary directory used. max_mutants Sample number mutants testing. NULL, mutants tested. timeout_seconds Optional timeout seconds mutant run. NULL, timeout derived baseline runtime small minimum floor. Still works compiled native code. config_dir Directory searched .openai_config file detectEqMutants = TRUE (see get_openai_config()). Defaults current working directory. max_line_deletions Maximum number line-deletion mutants per .R file (passed mutate_file()); 0 disables . Defaults 0, since line-deletion mutants largely redundant AST block-deletion mutants generated default. cran Logical; TRUE (default), tests run "CRAN mode": NOT_CRAN environment variable set "false" test subprocess testthat::skip_on_cran() / skip_if_offline() guards take effect tests CRAN run used (skipping network/slow tests package marks). Set FALSE run full suite (NOT_CRAN = "true"), devtools::test() . fail_fast Logical; TRUE (default), mutant's test run stops first failing test rather running whole suite. mutant KILLED soon one test detects , remainder suite wasted work. Set FALSE run full suite every mutant. Applies testthat strategy; installed-tests fallback already stops first failing test file regardless flag. isolate Logical; FALSE (default), mutant's package copy symlinks unchanged directories original package (mutated R/ file materialised), fast makes directories shared writable state across parallel workers. TRUE, src/ tests/ directories deep-copied every mutant copy instead. Use isolate = TRUE package non-hermetic tests write files tests/ (src/) parallel runs therefore produce spurious KILLED/HANG verdicts; gives worker copy cost extra disk. Note running cores = 1 avoids contention without copy cost. exclude_files Optional character vector shell-style glob patterns (e.g. "import-standalone-*") matched base names .R files R/. Matching files skipped entirely mutants generated. NULL (default) mutates every file. complements -source # mutator:ignore-file # mutator:ignore-start / # mutator:ignore-end directives, exclude whole file line region within source . Note operator mutations engine resolves positions enclosing top-level definition, region directive excludes function's operator mutants group (line-deletion mutants excluded line-precisely). strategy Test strategy use. "auto" (default) picks testthat strategy tests/testthat/ exists installed-tests strategy otherwise. "testthat" forces -process testthat::test_dir() path (requires tests/testthat/). "installed" forces R CMD INSTALL --install-tests + tools::testInstalledPackage() path (requires tests/). coverage_guided Logical; TRUE, tests actually exercise mutant's mutated line(s) run mutant, instead whole suite. Coverage measured unmutated package covr (options(covr.record_tests = TRUE)). mutant line test covers killed, reported SURVIVED without running test. Selection test-file level (testthat filters file); assumption suite deterministically exercises code, change mutant's verdict, tests run. Defaults TRUE. Coverage guidance available testthat strategy; resolved strategy installed-tests fallback, mutator emits warning runs full suite every mutant. Pass FALSE disable (silence warning). coverage_backend coverage_guided attributes coverage tests (ignored coverage_guided = FALSE). "record_tests" (default) uses covr's record_tests single run; relies covr's public output , covr credits covered line deepest test-directory frame, code reached helper-*.R/setup-*.R wrapper attributed helper rather originating test-*.R file, mutants conservatively run whole suite. "per_file" instruments package runs suite single time reporter snapshots coverage per test file, giving exact file-level attribution (helper fallback) roughly cost; depends covr internals, opt-. target_margin Optional desired half-width confidence interval mutation score, proportion (e.g. 0.05 +/-5 percentage points). set, number mutants sample derived using worst-case (p = 0.5) sizing confidence, finite-population corrected capped number mutants generated (requested precision needs mutants exist, tested). Mutually exclusive max_mutants. required sample size depends target precision, program size (Gopinath et al., ISSRE 2015). confidence Confidence level target_margin sizing Wilson confidence interval reported sampled mutation score. Default 0.95. max_show Maximum number surviving mutants print console; remainder summarised "... N " always remain returned package_mutants. Use Inf print every survivor. Default 50.

title: Run Mutation Testing for an R Package — mutate_package
text: invisible list four components: package_mutants Named list mutant path, mutation info, status, optional equivalence flags. test_results Named list mapping mutant IDs statuses: "KILLED", "SURVIVED", "HANG". timing Named list phase durations seconds: baseline, generation, test_execution, equivalence_detection. summary Named list generated, tested, killed, hanged, survived, mutation_score, mutation_score_ci (length-2 percentage vector, NULL sampling occurred), confidence.

title: Run Mutation Testing for an R Package — mutate_package
text: Test strategy , default, detected automatically: tests/testthat/ exists, mutant loaded -process pkgload::load_all() (installation) tests run way package's tests/testthat.R harness runs , .e. arguments (notably filter) harness passes testthat::test_check(), via testthat::test_dir(). Otherwise, tests/ exists, mutator installs mutant package --install-tests runs tools::testInstalledPackage(). Pass strategy override (example run testthat package slower installed-tests path comparison).

title: Run Mutation Testing for an R Package — mutate_package
code: # Wrapped in \donttest{}: it loads and test-runs a throwaway package, which # is too slow/heavy for routine automated checks. # \donttest{ pkg <- file.path(tempdir(), "examplepkg") dir.create(file.path(pkg, "R"), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE) dir.create(file.path(pkg, "tests", "testthat"), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE) writeLines(c(   "Package: examplepkg",   "Title: Example Package",   "Version: 0.0.1",   "Description: Minimal package for a mutator example.",   "License: GPL-3",   "Encoding: UTF-8" ), file.path(pkg, "DESCRIPTION")) writeLines("export(add)", file.path(pkg, "NAMESPACE")) writeLines("add <- function(x, y) x + y", file.path(pkg, "R", "add.R")) writeLines(   "testthat::expect_equal(add(1, 2), 3)",   file.path(pkg, "tests", "testthat", "test-add.R") ) result <- mutate_package(pkg, cores = 1, max_mutants = 1, timeout_seconds = 10) #> Generated 1 AST-based mutants for add.R #> Generated 1 mutants from 1 source files. #> Running the test suites of 1 mutant... #>  #> Surviving mutants (1): #>   R/add.R:1   '+' -> '-' #>     > 1 | add <- function(x, y) x + y #> Timing (seconds): #>   Baseline run:          1.1 #>   Mutant generation:     0.0 #>   Test execution:        1.0 #>   Equivalence detection: 0.0 #>  #> Mutation Testing Summary: #>   Total mutants:    1 #>   Killed:           0 #>   Hanged:           0 #>   Survived:         1 #>   Mutation Score:   0.00% names(result) #> [1] "package_mutants" "test_results"    "timing"          "summary"         # }

what: Mutation Operators Supported by mutator — operators
title: Mutation Operators Supported by mutator — operators
text: help page lists mutation operators mutator package supports mutation testing R.

title: Mutation Operators Supported by mutator — operators
text: following mutation operators can applied R code: Arithmetic operators: + <-> -, * <-> / Comparison operators: == <-> !=, < <-> >, <= <-> >= Logical operators: \& <-> |, && <-> ||, removes !, negates / conditions Assignment call values: replaces assignment right-hand sides ordinary function calls 42 Scalar constants: replaces numeric zero 42, numeric non-zero values 0, constants typed NA, constants NULL Returns: replaces non-constant direct return() values NULL, example return(x) -> return(NULL) Deletions: removes statements inside { ... } blocks , fallback, valid source lines Direct literal return values rewritten return--NULL mutation; example, return(1) left alone mutation.

title: Mutation Operators Supported by mutator — operators
text: Assanali Amandykov Pierre Donat-Bouillud

title: Set OpenAI API configuration for the current session — set_openai_config

code: set_openai_config(   api_key = NULL,   model = NULL,   base_url = NULL,   max_parallel_requests = NULL )

title: Set OpenAI API configuration for the current session — set_openai_config
text: api_key API key string. model Model name (e.g. "gpt-4"). base_url Base URL OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions API, "https://api.openai.com/v1" another provider endpoint. max_parallel_requests Maximum number equivalence-detection API requests run concurrently. Use stay provider's per-key parallel-request limit (exceeding returns HTTP 429). NA (default) imposes cap beyond run's cores.

title: Set OpenAI API configuration for the current session — set_openai_config
